chunk_summary: This section discusses implementation research designs for nutrition practice, emphasizing design choices, reporting standards, and dual outcomes to ensure effective and replicable interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the application of PDSA cycles, pragmatic trials, and stepped-wedge cluster designs in nutrition practice.
Learn to define and track dual outcomes, including clinical and implementation metrics.
Explore reporting standards like TIDieR and StaRI for replicable implementation studies.
key_facts: PDSA cycles are ideal for rapid iteration within a single clinic or ward.
PRECIS-2 tool helps specify the pragmatism of trials for real-world effectiveness.
Dual outcomes include clinical metrics (e.g., BMI trajectory, hemoglobin change) and implementation metrics (e.g., reach, fidelity, sustainability).
TIDieR checklist ensures full intervention description, including delivery, dose, and tailoring.
StaRI principles focus on reporting implementation strategies and context alongside outcomes.
reference_quote: Implementation outcomes taxonomy defines key metrics like reach, fidelity, acceptability, and sustainability to distinguish 'not delivered' from 'ineffective.' (Proctor et al.)
document_key_topics: Implementation research designs for nutrition practice
Dual outcomes in clinical and implementation metrics
